perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means, for each party, whether in oral, written or in electronic form: (i) any information, data, intellectual property (including trade secrets), facts and financial information relating to the existing or proposed business, products, customers or services of such party and its affiliates that are treated as confidential by it, or ought to be considered confidential from their nature or the circumstances surrounding their disclosure; (ii) the existence of, and either party's potential participation in, the Proposed Transaction (including the contents and details of any discussions and negotiations of the terms, conditions and other matters with respect thereto); and (iii) any reports, analyses, forecasts, memoranda, compilations, studies, notes or summaries of such information, or other written or electronic documents prepared by, or on behalf of or for the Recipient or affiliates, directors, officers, managers, employees, shareholders, members or other equity holders, agents or advisors (“Representatives”) of the Recipient, to the extent that they use, contain, relate to, reflect, or are derived from such information (collectively, “Derivative Confidential Information”).
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means, for each party, whether in oral, written or in electronic form: (i) any information, data, intellectual property (including trade secrets), facts and financial information relating to the existing or proposed business, products, customers or services of such party and its affiliates that are treated as confidential by it; (ii) the existence of, and either party's potential participation in, the Proposed Transaction (including the contents and details of any discussions and negotiations of the terms, conditions and other matters with respect thereto); and (iii) any reports, analyses, forecasts, memoranda, compilations, studies, notes or summaries of such information, or other written or electronic documents prepared by, or on behalf of or for the Recipient or affiliates, directors, officers, managers, employees, shareholders, members or other equity holders, agents or advisors (“Representatives”) of the Recipient, to the extent that they use, contain, relate to, reflect, or are derived from such information (collectively, “Derivative Confidential Information”).
perturbation.explanation: This change removes 'or ought to be considered confidential from their nature or the circumstances surrounding their disclosure' from the definition of Confidential Information. This introduces ambiguity because the definition now only includes information explicitly treated as confidential. This contradicts section 9 which implies that some information, by its nature, is considered confidential regardless of explicit treatment, potentially leading to disputes over what constitutes Confidential Information and therefore what obligations apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information will remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Confidential Information will be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and its Representatives. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out in this Agreement, is granted to the Recipient under this Agreement by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes information that 'ought to be considered confidential from their nature', while the changed text removes this. The contradicted text states that Confidential Information 'will be held in trust and confidence', implying a responsibility for all information considered confidential. By removing the 'ought to be considered confidential' clause, the modified text creates a contradiction by narrowing the scope of what is considered Confidential Information, thus potentially excluding information that should be held in confidence according to the contradicted section. This creates ambiguity and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, information which would otherwise be considered “Confidential Information”, is not subject to the terms of this Agreement if such information is: (a) in the public domain other than as a consequence of a breach of the obligations contained in this Agreement or other duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information; (b) known by the party receiving the Confidential Information (the “Recipient”) prior to the disclosure of the Confidential Information by the disclosing party (the “Disclosing Party”) without any other obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (c) independently developed by the Recipient without breach of this Agreement or other duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information; or (d) received by the Recipient from a third party who is not subject to obligations similar to the obligations contained in this Agreement, including any duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, information which would otherwise be considered “Confidential Information”, is not subject to the terms of this Agreement if such information is: (a) in the public domain; (b) known by the party receiving the Confidential Information (the “Recipient”) prior to the disclosure of the Confidential Information by the disclosing party (the “Disclosing Party”); (c) independently developed by the Recipient; or (d) received by the Recipient from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the qualifying phrases 'other than as a consequence of a breach of the obligations contained in this Agreement or other duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information', 'without any other obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party', 'without breach of this Agreement or other duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information', and 'who is not subject to obligations similar to the obligations contained in this Agreement, including any duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party relating to such information' from the exceptions to Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because section 3 states the Recipient must keep the information confidential, but now information leaked due to Recipient's breach might not be considered confidential anymore, thus reducing the level of protection to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person except Recipient’s Representatives, in each case, who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. In any such case Confidential Information will be disclosed only to those Representatives with a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, and (iv) only so much of the Confidential Information as is necessary for a particular individual to perform his or her function will be disclosed to that individual. Under no circumstances shall the Recipient or its Representatives use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any manner or for any purpose other than evaluating and negotiating the Proposed Transaction. In any event, the Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations by removing key exceptions that previously protected the Disclosing Party. Specifically, the original text ensured that information leaked due to a breach by the Recipient remained confidential. The changed text removes this protection, directly contradicting the Contradicted Text, which states the Recipient will keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and is responsible for breaches. The removal of the qualifying phrases creates a direct conflict and ambiguity, making it unclear whether information leaked due to the Recipient's breach is still considered confidential, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person except Recipient’s Representatives, in each case, who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. In any such case Confidential Information will be disclosed only to those Representatives with a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, and (iv) only so much of the Confidential Information as is necessary for a particular individual to perform his or her function will be disclosed to that individual. Under no circumstances shall the Recipient or its Representatives use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any manner or for any purpose other than evaluating and negotiating the Proposed Transaction. In any event, the Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will reasonably keep, and will cause its Representatives to reasonably keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person except Recipient’s Representatives, in each case, who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. In any such case Confidential Information will be disclosed only to those Representatives with a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, and (iv) only so much of the Confidential Information as is necessary for a particular individual to perform his or her function will be disclosed to that individual. Under no circumstances shall the Recipient or its Representatives use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any manner or for any purpose other than evaluating and negotiating the Proposed Transaction. In any event, the Recipient will be responsible for any gross breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'reasonably' and changing 'any breach' to 'any gross breach', the agreement now introduces a subjective element to the confidentiality obligation and the liability of Recipient for Representative breaches. This contradicts the strict liability implied in section 9, where the Confidential Information 'will be held in trust and confidence'. The term 'gross breach' is undefined, allowing for potentially numerous interpretations, leading to conflicting interpretations of obligations by the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information will remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and the Confidential Information will be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and its Representatives. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out in this Agreement, is granted to the Recipient under this Agreement by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Recipient strictly liable for 'any breach' by its Representatives. The changed text alters this to 'any gross breach', and adds 'reasonably keep' which introduces a subjective element to the confidentiality obligation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Confidential Information 'will be held in trust and confidence' by the Recipient, implying a strict obligation. The subjective standard introduced by 'reasonably' and 'gross breach' creates a clear conflict with the strict obligation outlined in the contradicted text, aligning with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction and causing uncertainty and potentially conflicting interpretations of obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees to take all reasonable steps (including those steps that the Recipient takes to protect its own information, data or other tangible or intangible property that it regards as confidential) to ensure the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to take steps (including those steps that the Recipient takes to protect its own information, data or other tangible or intangible property) to ensure the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "reasonable", section 4 now requires the Recipient to take all possible steps, regardless of cost or feasibility, to protect Confidential Information. This contradicts the provision in section 12 which states that the Disclosing Party provides the information without liability. If compliance with this section becomes extraordinarily costly or burdensome, it creates an in-text contradiction with section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient acknowledges that (i) the Disclosing Party is not obligated to provide any information pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) the Confidential Information is being given to it without liability on the part of the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, and (iii) no representation or warranty with respect to the Confidential Information is made by the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives. The Recipient agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives will have any liability to the Recipient or its Representatives resulting from the use of the Confidential Information by the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'reasonable steps' to ensure confidentiality, aligning with section 12's 'without liability' clause. The changed text removes 'reasonable', now demanding all possible steps regardless of cost or feasibility. This directly contradicts section 12, as the recipient could incur significant liability protecting the disclosing party's information, undermining the 'without liability' provision. The change introduces a direct conflict between the level of protection required and the liability disclaimer, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate on the first anniversary of the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate on the first anniversary of the date hereof, unless both parties agree to extend it in writing, in which case it will terminate on the second anniversary.
perturbation.explanation: This adds a clause which allows for a one-time extension of the agreement. This introduces ambiguity because section 11, which defines the return or destruction of confidential information, specifies that these obligations apply 'within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party'. The extension now makes unclear when the five days start: after the first anniversary (if no extension is agreed), or after the second anniversary (if there's an extension).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will, within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, confirm that it has destroyed the original provided to Recipient (and all copies made by it) any documents (in whatever Project Bell – Non-disclosure Agreement Page 3 of 6 form or medium) provided, or made available for review by, the Disclosing Party (including any Derivative Confidential Information). Notwithstanding the immediately preceding sentence, the parties agree that destruction will not be made of (i) any electronic copy of any Confidential Information that was created pursuant to the standard electronic backup and archival procedures of the Recipient if (x) personnel of the Recipient whose functions are not primarily internal information technology in nature do not have access to such retained copies and (y) personnel of the Recipient whose functions are primarily internal information technology in nature have access to such copies only as reasonably necessary for the performance of their internal information technology duties ( e.g. , for purposes of system recovery). The parties further agree that they will continue to treat as confidential in accordance with the Agreement, any Confidential Information retained pursuant to the second sentence of this Section 11.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an extension clause, creating ambiguity about the agreement's termination date. The contradicted text specifies that confidential information must be returned within five business days of a written request. The changed text makes it unclear whether the five-day period starts after the original termination date or the extended one, creating a direct conflict and therefore uncertainty. The ambiguity directly contradicts the specific timeline set out in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement is distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter. No amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding unless executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement is distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter, except for prior oral agreements between the parties pertaining to non-disclosure. No amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding unless executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds the phrase 'except for prior oral agreements between the parties pertaining to non-disclosure' This directly contradicts the assertion that 'This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter.' This implies that prior oral agreements, which are obviously not written, pertaining to non-disclosure are still valid, therefore creating an ambiguity about which non-disclosure rules and obligations are relevant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement is distinct and severable and a declaration of invalidity or unenforceability of any provision by a court of competent jurisdiction will not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties pertaining to its subject matter, except for prior oral agreements between the parties pertaining to non-disclosure. No amendment or waiver of this Agreement will be binding unless executed in writing by the party to be bound.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the 'entire agreement' clause, stating that prior oral agreements regarding non-disclosure are still valid. This directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement constitutes the 'entire agreement,' creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction arises because the original text asserts that the written agreement encompasses all understandings, while the changed text acknowledges the potential validity of separate, unwritten agreements. As the Changed Text includes information that directly contradicts the original statement of the Agreement, this is a strong contradiction.
